phases.name: philosophy show resolves aliases to the canonical record

steps.name: resolve name|object|alias in the pure lexicon layer and drive `philosophy show` on it
steps.description: Today `openlore philosophy show <key>` resolves a bare name or a derived object id via lexicon::philosophy::find, but an ALIAS (e.g. `xp`, `ci`, `tdd`) misses — `show xp` prints `no such philosophy`. US-PV-002's promise is that a user can inspect a philosophy by any string that triangulates onto it. Add a pure, total lexicon::philosophy::resolve(key) that layers alias resolution on top of find (reusing resolve_object_advisory + object_id — no second copy of the NSID prefix or resolution logic), and point the CLI show verb at resolve instead of find. find keeps its tight contract (Some => name/object-id match) so its soundness property test is untouched; resolve is the wider name-or-object-OR-alias resolver.
criteria: OWNED resolve_finds_every_seed_by_name_object_id_and_alias (every seed resolves by name, object-id, and each alias — bare, object-id form, and case-insensitively)
OWNED resolve_is_total_and_sound_over_arbitrary_input (never panics; a hit genuinely names the key by name/object/alias; a miss means no seed matched)
ACCEPTANCE philosophy_show_by_alias_renders_the_canonical_record (CLI `show mem-safety` exits 0 and renders the memory-safety record)
GUARDRAIL the slice-23 philosophy_show acceptance scenarios (by-name, by-object-id, unknown-name, network-disabled) stay green
steps.implementation_notes: Pure resolver: resolve(key) = find(key), else prefix the key into the philosophy namespace if bare (object_id) and classify via resolve_object_advisory; an Alias{canonical} verdict resolves to find(canonical); anything else -> None. Total and offline (embedded seeds only). CLI change is a one-line swap of `find` for `resolve` in philosophy_show::run — the render/guidance paths are unchanged, so the unknown-name sad path and its plain guidance stay identical. No crate/dep/route change; check-arch stays at 21 members. Demo: openlore philosophy show xp.

validation.reviewer: orchestrator (user-directed follow-on slice) + automated quality gate
validation.review_verdict: APPROVED — single-step slice. RED demonstrated via stub before GREEN; 2 owned pure pins + 1 CLI acceptance scenario; find contract preserved. Reuses existing resolution seams (no duplicated NSID prefix).
